---
title: "Solution to Quarto task"
code-line-numbers: false
---

Below is an example solution for the Quarto task. Your document will look different depending on the text you wrote and the plots you chose — that's fine! The important thing is that you have all the elements working.

## The complete document

Here is the full source of the example document. Copy it into a `.qmd` file and render it to see the result.

````md
---
title: "Penguin Report"
author: "Selina Baldauf"
format: html
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{{r}}
#| include: false
library(tidyverse)
```

## Introduction

This is a short report about the **Palmer Penguins** dataset.
The data contains measurements for three penguin species:

- *Adelie*
- *Chinstrap*
- *Gentoo*

You can find more information about the data on the
[palmerpenguins website](https://allisonhorst.github.io/palmerpenguins/).

## Data overview

```{{r}}
head(penguins)
```

The dataset contains `r knitr::inline_expr("nrow(penguins)")` penguins
from `r knitr::inline_expr("length(unique(penguins$species))")` species.

## Analysis

```{{r}}
#| echo: false
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = bill_len, y = bill_dep, color = species)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = "Bill length (mm)",
    y = "Bill depth (mm)",
    color = "Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{{r}}
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, y = body_mass, fill = species)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Species", y = "Body mass (g)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
````

## Explanation of the key elements

### YAML header

```yaml
---
title: "Penguin Report"
author: "Selina Baldauf"
format: html
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---
```

- `title`, `author`: appear at the top of the rendered document
- `format: html`: renders to an HTML file
- `execute`: sets default chunk options for the whole document. `warning: false` and `message: false` suppress warnings and messages in all chunks (unless overwritten in a specific chunk)

### Chunk options

- `#| include: false`: runs the code but **hides everything** (code, output, warnings, messages) from the document. Useful for setup chunks.
- `#| echo: false`: runs the code and shows the **output** but hides the **code**. Useful for showing plots without the code that created them.

### Inline code

`` `r knitr::inline_expr("nrow(penguins)")` `` inserts the result of the R expression directly into the text. The value updates automatically when the data changes.
